200.reason: [Error: The code does not handle comments. When encountering "//", it should skip the rest of the line instead of treating each '/' as a DIV token. This causes the buggy output to include extra tokens for the comment content.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The code does not handle comments. When encountering "//", it should skip the rest of the line instead of treating each '/' as a DIV token. This causes the buggy output to include extra tokens for the comment content.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line generates a DIV token for '/', which is incorrect when '/' is part of a comment. The logic needs to be updated to distinguish between division operators and comment starters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line unconditionally adds a DIV token, even when the '/' is part of a comment '//'.

201.reason: [Error: The '/' character is not checked for comments (// or /* */). In the test input, "// 输出 1" is a comment, but the lexer treats each '/' as a DIV token, causing the comment content to be parsed incorrectly as identifiers and numbers.]

199.reason: The code incorrectly treats '/' as a DIV token immediately without checking for comments. It should check if the next character is '/' or '*' to handle single-line or multi-line comments, respectively. If it is a comment, the lexer should skip the comment content instead of generating a DIV token.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The code incorrectly treats '/' as a DIV token immediately without checking for comments. It should check if the next character is '/' or '*' to handle single-line or multi-line comments, respectively. If it is a comment, the lexer should skip the comment content instead of generating a DIV token.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The code immediately increments location and creates a DIV token without checking if the next character is also '/', which fails to handle single-line comments starting with '//'. This causes the subsequent characters in the comment (like '输出') to be tokenized incorrectly.

215.reason: [This handles '/' as DIV but fails to recognize '//' as the start of a comment; as a result, the second '/' and subsequent characters (like "输出 1") are incorrectly lexed as tokens instead of being skipped as part of a comment.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This handles '/' as DIV but fails to recognize '//' as the start of a comment; as a result, the second '/' and subsequent characters (like "输出 1") are incorrectly lexed as tokens instead of being skipped as part of a comment.]
